title: NIS2 Secure System Acquisition and Development Lifecycle

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(e) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) (Security in acquisition, development and maintenance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.15: NIS2 Secure System Acquisition and Development Lifecycle.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.15' running on index devops and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=devops` (CI/CD pipeline events from GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins), `index=codescan` (SAST/SCA scan results), `index=itsm` (change management records)
app: Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596), Splunk Add-on for Jira (Splunkbase 1438)
spl:
index=devops sourcetype IN ("github:webhook","gitlab:pipeline","jenkins:build") earliest=-30d
| eval has_security_scan=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)sast|sca|snyk|sonar|trivy|semgrep|security.scan|code.scan"), 1, 0)
| eval deployment=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)deploy|release|prod|production"), 1, 0)
| stats count as total_builds, sum(has_security_scan) as scanned_builds, sum(deployment) as deployments by repository
| eval scan_coverage=round(100*scanned_builds/total_builds, 1)
| where scan_coverage < 80 OR (deployments > 0 AND scanned_builds = 0)
| sort scan_coverage
| table repository, total_builds, scanned_builds, scan_coverage, deployments
description: Article 21(2)(e) extends beyond vulnerability management to require security in system acquisition, development, and maintenance lifecycles. This use case monitors CI/CD pipeline security gates, tracks code scanning results, and validates that change management processes include security review — demonstrating that security is embedded in the development lifecycle rather than bolted on after deployment.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes codescan, devops, itsm and sourcetypes github:webhook, gitlab:pipeline, jenkins:build. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Forward CI/CD pipeline events to Splunk via webhooks or HEC; (2) define minimum security gate requirements (SAST scan, dependency check, container image scan); (3) alert on deployments to production without security scan evidence; (4) track scan coverage percentage as a NIS2 KPI; (5) correlate with change management tickets to verify security review sign-off. Indexes required: `index=codescan`, `index=devops`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=github:webhook`, `sourcetype=gitlab:pipeline`, `sourcetype=jenkins:build`.
visualization: Table (repos without scans), Bar chart (scan coverage by repo), Single value (overall scan coverage %), Line chart (coverage trend). Key fields: `deployment`, `deployments`, `has_security_scan`, `repository`, `scan_coverage`, `scanned_builds`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for article 21(2)(e) extends beyond vulnerability management to require security in system acquisition, development, and maintenance lifecycles. this use case monitors ci/cd pipeline security gates, tr. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596), Splunk Add-on for Jira (Splunkbase 1438).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=devops` (CI/CD pipeline events from GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins), `index=codescan` (SAST/SCA scan results), `index=itsm` (change management records).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Forward CI/CD pipeline events to Splunk via webhooks or HEC; (2) define minimum security gate requirements (SAST scan, dependency check, container image scan); (3) alert on deployments to production without security scan evidence; (4) track scan coverage percentage as a NIS2 KPI; (5) correlate with change management tickets to verify security review sign-off.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype IN ("github:webhook","gitlab:pipeline","jenkins:build") earliest=-30d
| eval has_security_scan=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)sast|sca|snyk|sonar|trivy|semgrep|security.scan|code.scan"), 1, 0)
| eval deployment=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)deploy|release|prod|production"), 1, 0)
| stats count as total_builds, sum(has_security_scan) as scanned_builds, sum(deployment) as deployments by repository
| eval scan_coverage=round(100*scanned_builds/total_builds, 1)
| where scan_coverage < 80 OR (deployments > 0 AND scanned_builds = 0)
| sort scan_coverage
| table repository, total_builds, scanned_builds, scan_coverage, deployments
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Secure System Acquisition and Development Lifecycle (Art. 21(2)(e))** — Article 21(2)(e) extends beyond vulnerability management to require security in system acquisition, development, and maintenance lifecycles. This use case monitors CI/CD pipeline security gates, tracks code scanning results, and validates that change management processes include security review — demonstrating that security is embedded in the development lifecycle rather than bolted on after deployment.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=devops` (CI/CD pipeline events from GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins), `index=codescan` (SAST/SCA scan results), `index=itsm` (change management records). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596), Splunk Add-on for Jira (Splunkbase 1438). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=devops, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **has_security_scan** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **deployment** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **repository**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **scan_coverage** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where scan_coverage < 80 OR (deployments > 0 AND scanned_builds = 0)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: deployment, deployments, has_security_scan, repository, scan_coverage, scanned_builds, total_builds.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (repos without scans), Bar chart (scan coverage by repo), Single value (overall scan coverage %), Line chart (coverage trend).
